title: Hang Lay Curry Paste

overview.description: A fragrant Northern Thai curry paste with Burmese influences, made from dried chillies, aromatic herbs and toasted spices. Freeze in portions for authentic Hang Lay curry.

ingredients: 10 dried long red chillies, deseeded and soaked in warm water for 20 minutes
2 stalks lemongrass, tender inner part only, sliced
method: Drain the soaked chillies, reserving the soaking water.
Toast the coriander seeds, cumin seeds and white peppercorns in a dry frying pan over a medium heat for 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Grind the toasted spices to a fine powder.
Place the chillies, shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, lemongrass, coriander stems, shrimp paste, turmeric, ground spices and vegetable oil into a food processor or mini chopper.
Blend until a smooth paste forms.
Scrape down the sides as needed.
Add the reserved soaking water one tablespoon at a time only if required to help the paste blend.
Use immediately or divide into portions and freeze.

learning.did-you-know: Hang Lay curry paste reflects centuries of cultural exchange between Northern Thailand and neighbouring Myanmar, making it quite different from Thailand's coconut-based curry pastes.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to prepare dried chillies, toast spices and build a deeply aromatic curry paste suitable for freezing.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A freezer staple that makes authentic Northern Thai Hang Lay curry practical for everyday cooking.
chapman-tips: Freeze in approximately 2 tablespoon portions—one portion makes a family curry for four.
Coriander stems provide excellent flavour while reducing waste.
Adjust the heat by increasing or decreasing the number of dried chillies.
The paste also works well as a marinade for pork before slow cooking.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze for up to 6 months in silicone ice cube trays or small containers.
notes.leftovers: Can be used to flavour stews or as a rub for slow-roasted pork.

similar-recipes: Gaeng Hang Lay (Northern Thai Pork Curry)
